matches.moment: Doué's introduction breaks Morocco's low block for France's second after Mbappé's opener, with Rahimi snatching a stoppage-time consolation on the counter to keep Morocco's resistance on record.

verdict: France enter this quarterfinal with the tournament's most statistically dominant profile - five knockout-and-group wins, two goals conceded, and a +5.8 xG overperformance that marks either generational finishing or a correction waiting to happen against the first genuinely well-organized defense they've faced since the group stage. Morocco offer that defense: a side that has conceded from quality (Brazil, Netherlands) but never been blown out, and that has found a goal in every match it has played, even with 2022's semifinal humbling still on the record. The absence of Saibari removes Morocco's most proven scoring thread and tilts this toward a French win, but the specific, opponent-matched evidence - not the aggregate season totals - argues against a comfortable French coast, and for a match that stays competitive into the second half before Mbappé and France's bench depth settle it. A repeat of 2022's final scoreline is the base case; a repeat of 2022's final margin is not.

players.name: Kylian Mbappé
players.stat: 2.90 xG, 6 goals, 21 shots (13 on target) this tournament
players.watch: Volume matters more than one moment - 5 big chances already missed

players.name: Soufiane Rahimi
players.stat: Goal + assist vs Haiti, 90+8' winner vs Canada; 12 goals in 37 caps
players.watch: Morocco's lone reliable outlet with Saibari ruled out injured

players.name: Achraf Hakimi
players.stat: One yellow card from missing a potential semifinal
players.watch: Discipline management could blunt his overlapping threat down the right
